{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

==External links== *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/otozukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/docs/hpg000001423.html Toki city official site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/otozukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/docs/hpg000001423.html Toki city official site] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/otozukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/docs/hpg000001423.html Toki city official site] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/otozukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/docs/hpg000001423.html Toki city official site] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. [[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q60769827}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q60769827}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]], Japan | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and [[:en:Chert|Chert]] stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist chapel]] in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. For many years, it was controlled by a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tokishi Station (Gifu)|Tokishi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist chapel}} in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. For many years, it was controlled by a local {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|en|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|WD=Q5359508|lt=Tokishi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Toki, Gifu|en|Toki, Gifu|WD=Q847332|lt=Toki, Gifu}}, Japan | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Toki, Gifu|en|Toki, Gifu|WD=Q847332|lt=Toki, Gifu}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and {{ill|Chert|en|Chert|WD=Q1070438|lt=Chert}} stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist chapel}} in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. For many years, it was controlled by a local {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|en|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|WD=Q5359508|lt=Tokishi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun]] {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient sites}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | native_name = 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ototsuka Kofun, Toki, 2019 - コピー.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|56|N|137|10|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Toki, Gifu|en|Toki, Gifu|WD=Q847332|lt=Toki, Gifu}}, Japan | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Otozuka Kofun - Danjirimaki Kofun'''|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun}} is a pair of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Izumi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Toki, Gifu|en|Toki, Gifu|WD=Q847332|lt=Toki, Gifu}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} of Japan. The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and {{ill|Chert|en|Chert|WD=Q1070438|lt=Chert}} stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist chapel}} in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. For many years, it was controlled by a local {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|en|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|WD=Q5359508|lt=Tokishi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/otozukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/docs/hpg000001423.html Toki city official site] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The two tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172125|title=乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳|trans-title=Otozuka kofun tsuketari Danjirimaki kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Otozuka kofun''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun'' located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the [[:en:Toki River|Toki River]] at an elevation of {{convert|150|m}} in the northern part of the city for Toki. It is the largest ''kofun'' in the city. It is {{convert|7.3|m}} on each side with a height of {{convert|6.6|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral stone-lined chamber facing south with a total length of {{convert|12.1|m}}. The burial chamber itself has dimensions of {{convert|5.2 x 2.6 x 2.7|m}} (length, width, height). The back wall of the burial chamber is a granite monolith, with dimensions of {{convert|2 x 2.7|m}}, and the walls are a mixture of granite and {{ill|Chert|en|Chert|WD=Q1070438|lt=Chert|1=Chert}} stones in three tiers. Huge monolithic stones were also used for the ceiling. From the construction method, it is believed that the tumulus dates from the middle of the seventh century. According to local tradition, this is the tomb of Oto-hime, the younger sister of Yasakarihime, consort to the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (71–130 AD).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb has been open since ancient times, and was used as a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist chapel|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] and as an air raid shelter in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. For many years, it was controlled by a local {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, but was purchased by the city of Toki. In 1988, the trees which had grown on the tumulus were cut down, revealing its true configuration, and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Toki City Board of Education from 2003 to 2006. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery were also discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Danjirimaki kofun''' is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located 70 meters to the northwest of the Otozuka Kofun. It has a diameter of 12.8 meters and height of 3.6 meters. The burial chamber is also a lateral passage opening to the south, with a total length of 8.7 meters. A monolith with a width of 1.7 meters was used for the back wall, and the side walls were lined with granite and chert in three layers. This tumulus is also thought to date from the first half to the middle of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|en|Tokishi Station (Gifu)|WD=Q5359508|lt=Tokishi Station|1=Tokishi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/otozukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/docs/hpg000001423.html Toki city official site] {{in lang|ja}}
